input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-12] A metal presser foot rests firmly on a piece of red patterned fabric characterized by a white geometric grid, which is layered over a white base material speckled with small orange dots. The silver needle assembly and bar remain completely stationary above the stitching line, showing no vertical oscillation or movement. Throughout the sequence, there is no observable change in the position of the machine components or the fabric layers; the materials do not advance or shift under the foot. The lighting conditions and the shadow cast on the right side remain consistent, indicating that the sewing machine is paused. The scene depicts a static moment where the machine holds the fabric steady without actively stitching or feeding the material forward.

[12-24] A hand enters from the left, using fingers to stabilize a piece of red fabric with a white geometric pattern layered over a white dotted background. The fingers press the material firmly near the silver presser foot. The sewing machine needle remains in a stationary, lowered position throughout, pinning the fabric layers without active stitching. The machine appears paused as the operator manually holds and slightly adjusts the red fabric's position under the presser foot, preventing any fabric feed or needle movement.

[24-30] A hand is visible on the left, fingers pressing a red fabric featuring a white geometric grid pattern against a white base layer with small dots. The sewing machine's silver needle remains fixed in the lowered position, securing the materials under the silver presser foot. The machine is paused, showing no stitching action or fabric feed, as the operator manually stabilizes the alignment.
[30-36] The hand withdraws from the frame, leaving the red patterned fabric and white dotted base stationary under the presser foot. The needle stays fixed in the down position without vertical oscillation. The scene remains completely static with no mechanical movement or fabric advancement, maintaining the paused state with consistent lighting on the machine components and materials.

The sewing machine remains paused with the needle fixed in the lowered position, securing the red geometric fabric against the white dotted base. The silver presser foot holds the stationary layers firmly. A finger enters the frame from the upper left, hovering near the fabric, before two fingers press firmly on the red geometric fabric near the silver presser foot to stabilize the material layers. The needle remains fixed in the lowered position, piercing the fabric without vertical oscillation or movement. The machine stays paused with no fabric feed or stitching action occurring as the operator's hand continues to hold the textile steady under the foot.

recent_thinks: [48-50] The fingers maintain their position pressing the red patterned fabric against the white dotted base near the silver presser foot. The needle remains stationary in the down position, securing the layers. No sewing motion occurs, and the fabric does not advance, indicating the machine remains paused while the operator stabilizes the material.

[50-52] Fingers continue pressing the red geometric fabric against the white dotted base near the silver presser foot. The needle remains fixed in the lowered position, securing the layers without vertical movement. The machine stays paused with no fabric feed occurring as the operator maintains manual stabilization of the textile under the foot.

[52-54] The sewing machine needle begins rapid vertical oscillation, piercing the red geometric fabric and white dotted base layers. The presser foot holds the material steady as the fabric feeds forward slightly under the stitching action. Fingers on the left guide the red textile, maintaining alignment while the machine actively sews along the curved edge.

The fabric layers advance steadily under the metal presser foot. Fingers remain positioned on the left, guiding the red textile along its curved edge as the machine actively sews.</think><action>silent</action>
